top_profane.text:
Okay, here's what you need to know about me to understand what a big deal this 5-star review is:

1) I have crap hair. Seriously. I know women always say they have crap hair, but I really do. My mom had baby-fine, very curly hair, and my dad had baby-fine very straight hair. So I have baby-fine hair that is curly underneath and straight on top. Even my stylist says that I have the most "challenging" hair she's ever worked with. ("Challenging" is her nice way of not saying "crappy".) Even my husband, who loves me very much, says he thinks I'm beautiful but he understands why I get frustrated with my hair. Seriously, it's a complete mess.

2) I'm 46 years old. That means I've been dealing with this crappy hair with more than three decades. I have tried everything: every product, every dryer, curler, straightener, brush, every cut -- with bangs, without, long, short, etc. You name it. I've tried it to make my hair less crappy. I have some skin in the game, and I'm no rookie.

3) As much as I have struggled with my hair, I am lazy at heart, and I DREAM of having hair I don't have to spend ridiculous amounts of time on just to make it look kind of normal. What I want, more than anything, is something that will be easy, and make my hair look like it's not a frizzy ball of crap. Any successes I have had were minimal at best, barring a product
top_profane.title: Oh. My. God.

First, I’ll give some quick notes I learned while putting it together so that you can quickly see them all in one place and don’t have to sludge through the rest of my review to see them. Then, I’ll give my in depth review if you want to read that.

QUICK NOTES FOR PUTTING IT TOGETHER:
⚫️The screws aren’t in a bag anywhere. They are in each end of the metal frames (total of 8 screws, 4 on each metal frame).
⚫️Mine didn’t come with the hexagon key it was supposed to come with. It only came with the small flat wrench. So, you might need your own screwdriver, but I’d recommend that anyways because for me screwdrivers are always easier than those darn hex keys!
⚫️ The instruction sheet doesn’t state which end of the side panels is up and which is down. I figured it out by putting my finger in the poles of the side panels. The ends that you could feel the threading in are the bottoms because you screw the wheels in there.
⚫️The 2 metal frames go between the side panels to hold them together. One goes at the top and one at the bottom. One metal frame is shaped kind of like a long rectangle that has 4 ends poking out the same distance, the other is kind of an H shape with 2 lines in the middle of the H. The H shaped goes on TOP, the other one goes on BOTTOM.
⚫️When you put one of the metal frames on and go to p
top_profane.title: FRUSTRATING to put together. Works well, but cheap. Definitely not worth $150

I got fed up with crappy clippers a while back, and I hit Amazon to pick up a premium pair. To hedge my bets, I ordered two: these, and the [[ASIN:B009WUA658 Tweezerman Deluxe Nail Clipper Set]]. I'm not going to lie: I'm a sucker for USA made stuff, and that's why I bought these. As much as I hate to admit it, I bought the Tweezerman set just in case these sucked because I trusted the brand, and I thought it was a surefire win.

Long story short: These put the Tweezerman to SHAME, and that's not to say the Tweezerman are a bad clipper by any measure -- quite the contrary, if I had opened them before this No-Mes, clipper, they would have (briefly) been the best I'd ever used. But these, well, they're just amazing.

I was beginning to think that I had nails made out of some x-men, superhuman supermaterial. I've seriously broken three pairs of clippers in the past year - two of them the 'hook' on the centerpost snapped off, and the third gave way at the bend for the cutting head. I've always had to crush my clippers with what seemed like an excessive amount of force, and in the end I'd be rewarded with a ragged, but shorter nail. No more- these cut like butter. I thought that maybe my nails were just a bit soft this time, so I tried my old set on the same nail, and it still needed tons of force. I also tried the Tweezerman clipper, and while it was extremely g
top_profane.title: The best clippers I've ever owned

This is my first time to ever write a review on a makeup product (even though I am a makeup junkie who has tried pretty much every product known to man over the years) so that alone should tell you just how impressed I am by this primer! Let me start by prefacing this with the fact I have extremely dry skin. It's been difficult to get used to considering the fact I had oily to oily/combination skin my entire life until I gave birth to baby #3 (2.5 years ago)! So now (at 34 years old) I have had to relearn my skin and find new products that work with dry skin! Along with all the changes to my skin I've also noticed my pores have, unfortunately, become even larger (if that's possible lol) and considerably more noticeable! If I want my makeup to go on even somewhat smoothly and to not look caked on or patchy I have to complete my normal skin care routine (making sure my face is as moisturized as possible) followed by applying a great primer! As far as primers go I feel like I've tried literally everything!

★Here's a list of a few primers I tried that I didn't have great results with:
-Smashbox Photo Finish
-NYX Pore Filler
-Benefit POREfessional Pore Minimizing
(original & hydrating)
-Urban Decay All Nighter
-Tarte Face Tape Primer
-Revlon Photo Ready
-Wet n Wild Prime Focus
-Maybeline Baby Skin Instant Pore Eraser

Morpho's lip stains are AMAZING; the ABSOLUTE BEST I have ever used! I seriously cannot rave enough about both Zinnia and Snap Dragon! (And Morpho's other products, lip glosses and lip pencils, are amazing too). EVERYTHING about these stains is FANTASTIC! And believe me, I've tried every lip stain "known to mankind"!!! I have tried everything from the cheap, drugstore brands to the more expensive, upscale/luxury lines.

I am very ill, with a debilitating combination of autoimmune diseases. I've been homebound and bedridden for years. Prior to getting sick, I never wore much makeup, except for lipstick. I've always loved wearing lip color. Even just a hint of color on the lips can really brighten up one's face and make a huge difference (especially when a chronic illness often times causes one to appear pale and sickly). Once I became bedridden, traditional lipstick was no longer an option. If I fall asleep in bed (or even if I'm awake, but just want to roll over and rest my head on the pillow), I don't want lipstick smeared all over my pillowcases and sheets (nor do I want it smeared all over my face). Lip stains became my answer and I won't ever go back to wearing lipstick again.The good stains (such as Morpho's) last all day and you never need to worry about the color transferring onto things (your teeth, cups/glasses, pillowcases, etc.) Lip stains are the way to go
top_rant.title: Zinnia is AMAZING! Absolute BEST lip stain ever!

I now have pretty prominent sun spots on my cheekbones from my younger years (If we only knew then what we know now). I also have some post acne marks - I get hormonal acne and when a pimple finally goes away it almost always leaves a red mark behind and it’s so hard to make them go away! I also have some scarring from picking at my pimples…again, from my younger years 🤦‍♀️ I know better now. Thankfully, pimple patches have became a thing and I can use those to stop me from picking. top_screaming.text:
I HOPE YOU WILL GET SOME GOOD SOLID INFO FROM MY REVIEW. FIRST, I WOULD LIKE TO SAY THAT I HAVE TRIED MANY ELECTRIC SHAVERS OVER THE PAST FEW WEEKS. I HATE SHAVING WITH A BLADE SO I WANTED TO FIND THE BEST SHAVER. I DO NOT WORK FOR PANASONIC NOR BRAUN. MY REVIEW IS HONEST AND I HAVE PUT ALOT OF TIME AND RESEARCH INTO THESE PRODUCTS, LOOKING FOR THE BEST SHAVER. I TESTED THE BRAUN PULSONIC 9595 SHAVER, AND ALSO THE PANASONIC VORTEX NANO ES 8109S FOR 30 DAYS. THE BRAUN I BOUGHT FOR 250 DOLLARS, THE PANASONIC VORTEX WAS ABOUT 130 DOLLARS. I ALSO USED THE PANASONIC 8077, WHICH IS DISCONTINUED AND ABOUT 40 DOLLARS ON THE INTERNET OR EBAY. I BOUGHT IT FROM SHARPER IMAGE FOR 99 DOLLARS MONTHS AGO. AGAIN, NOW WORTH ABOUT 40 BUCKS.

THE BRAUN DOES TONS OF MARKETING, MAKING BELIEVE THE PULSONIC MUST BE THE BEST SHAVER ON THE MARKET. IT IS A GREAT SHAVER AND REALLY WORKED PRETTY WELL. BUT THERE IS NO CONTEST. THE PANASONIC MODELS SHAVED MUCH CLOSER AND FOR ABOUT HALF THE PRICE. THE BRAUN DID OK ON THE NECK AREA BUT THE PANASONIC WON THE CONTEST. THE BRUAN WANTS YOU TO CLEAN THE UNIT EVERY FEW SHAVES, THAT GETS EXPENSIVE WHILE YOU MUST USE ITS CLEANING CARTRIDGE/SYSTEM. THE PANASONIC GIVES YOU THE SAME CLEANING SYSTEM BUT ALSO ALLOWS YOU TO USE THE WATER FROM YOUR SINK. ITS A WET AND DRY UNIT. THE PANASONIC BY FAR IS THE BETTER SHAVER AND MUCH CHEAPER PRICE WISE.

top_screaming.text: I WANTED TO TEACH MYSELF TO BRAID, SO I STARTED LOOKING FOR MANNEQUIN HEADS. AFTER READING MANY OF THE REVIEWS ABOUT THE HAIR SHEDDING AND NOT AS LONG AS THE PICTURE, I WAS A LITTLE HESITANT. HOWEVER, FOR THE PRICE AND TYPE OF HAIR I NEEDED, I TOOK A CHANCE. YES, IT SHEDS A LOT.....BUT, I'VE NOTICED THAT AFTER COMBING AND STYLING MULTIPLE TIMES (EVERY DAY ABOUT 4 TIMES A DAY, DOING BRAID STYLES FOR ABOUT A MONTH NOW SINCE I GOT IT) THE SHEDDING IS VERY MINIMAL. ALMOST LIKE IT HAS TO BE USED ENOUGH TO GET THAT SHEDDING STAGE OUT OF THE WAY. IT WILL ANNOY YOU AT FIRST,BUT IT DOES CEASE IF YOU'RE USING IT REGULARLY.  NOW, PEOPLE COMPLAINED ABOUT GREY HAIRS.  PERSONALLY, I JUST NEEDED HAIR TO BRAID, COLOR DID NOT MATTER. MY HEAD DID HAVE MAYBE 3-5 GREY HAIRS THROUGHOUT, BUT NOT A BIG DEAL TO ME AT ALL. IT WASN'T AN ISSUE AND IF YOU REALLY HATE THEM, YOU CAN CUT THEM OUT AND IT WOULDN'T AFFECT THE HAIR. THE SKIN TONE WAS A LITTLE DARKER THAN THE PICTURE, BUT I DIDN'T CARE. IT LOOKED EXACTLY LIKE HER FACE AND SKIN TONE HAD NOTHING TO DO WITH MY INTENTIONS. IF I NEEDED A MAKE-UP DOLL AND REQUIRED LIGHTER SKIN TONES, THEN I MIGHT BE A LITTLE MAD, BUT AGAIN, MY PURPOSE WAS STRICTLY LEARNING TO BRAID AND THE HAIR WAS MY FOCUS. THE HAIR REALLY IS A GOOD MATCH TO AFRICAN AMERICAN HAIR THAT'S BEEN BLOW DRIED OUT. SO, IF THAT'S THE TYPE OF HAIR YOU'RE LOOKING FOR, IT'S PRETTY CLOSE. THAT'S R
top_screaming.title: GLAD I DECIDED TO TAKE A CHANCE!

top_exclaim.text:
My fiancé and I occasionally use the same shave cream/gel etcetera depending on various conditions. For the past 6 years I have been using Jack Black’s Supreme Cream to shave my legs, but recently I decided to try something a little less pricey...
You get what you pay for!
Not only did this item come with the box completely destroyed (basically desintigrated, like it was soaked through and run through a food processor) the tube was unsealed, no little foil button to confirm that I was the first to open the container.
My fiancé, whose skin is no where near as sensitive as mine tried it out first. Not very thick lather, even with a shave brush. Did not rinse clean, he said he used more hot water rincing his razor than he uses for a whole shower (surely an exaggeration).
However, after trying it on my legs I have to confirm that it does not cushion as Supreme Cream does, it does not rinse clean as supreme cream does, and it does not even come close to the lather WITHOUT a shave brush that supreme cream does.
Another HUGE letdown was the packaging. I’ll admit that if the box had been smashed, that’s okay, but if I were giving it as a gift, totally unacceptable. As for no seal on the tube....that’s unacceptable. Also, when I tried to return the tube I was told not to return the product and that I would receive a full refund. Since when is $5.36 a full refund
top_exclaim.title: Negative 5 stars !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!WORST SHAVE CREAM EVER!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

top_five_star_obscene.text: It is so difficult to get chlorine out of thick hair and actually takes me a couple of days to finally not smell it. This shampoo is a wonderful invention. It strips your hair of all of the crap (which is something we should do anyway once in a while...but not too often or your hair will be crappy) and the chlorine is absolutely gone! I wasn't a big fan of the smell but what can you do? So I used it and then used my own shampoo afterward. Then I used the Ultra Swim Conditioner, which is a must because you have to put the nutrients back into your hair that it needs after stripping it down. The product information says it may take a man a few weeks to "adjust" to the shaving, but my man had no problem like that all, no chaffing, no problem, just a beautiful smooth head and face. He usually has it in his truck and can use it driving down the road on his way to a job. The battery life is amazing, he can go days on a charge!

I can't think of any complaints, he loves it, he loves me for buying it for him, what's to complain about? He hasn't tried the attachments yet (you can buy clippers and nose picker), but probably will buy them soon to try.

I bought the Intensive Therapy at a big box store that starts with W, fell in love with it, came to Amazon to check out other items from the brand. This duo cost me less than the single bottle did at the store. I'm either giving my mom my extra IT or I'm saving it.

Background: I have extremely THIN peeling nails because of nutrient deficiencies that I need to work on. I do my nails a lot cause it's my passion, so my nails go through a lot of crap. But even when I take long hiatuses from painting them, they are still awful. I paint my nails 1-3 times a week, sometimes more, sometimes less. I've been using the IT for about 3 weeks, Foundation for about a week. My nails are about as bend-resistant as a generic solo cup if that makes sense. This is important to take note of.

First lets talk about Intensive Therapy. WOW this stuff is great. After 2 or 3 uses, I noticed my nails were growing like crazy, polishes were applying smoother, and my nails started to feel thicker. They're starting to feel like real nails. I'm amazed. I have tried practically every strengthening base out there with no results. I will never stop using this stuff. I apply it 1-2 times a day if I'm not wearing regular polish. If I am, I apply a coat over my polish every day like the box says. The key to this stuff is CONSISTENCY. Use it a lot!

Now for Foundation. I love it also.
